notes: PEGylated Au NP. 0.6% translocation. Low membrane retention. Stable in media.
Carboxylated Au NP. Only 0.1% translocation. 77.4% membrane retention. 1370 ug/g by LA-ICP-MS. Agglomerates in media.
PEGylated Au NP perfusion. Only NP detected in fetal circuit (0.01%). Tissue: 7.9 ug/g. Stable.
Carboxylated Au NP perfusion. NOT detected in fetal circuit. Tissue: 2-14 ug/g. Adhered to device.
Plain PS ~80nm. ~3% translocation to fetal circuit. Size-dependent transfer confirmed.
Plain PS ~500nm. ~1.5% translocation. Larger particles less transfer.
COOH-modified ~100nm. ~2% translocation. Surface charge did not prevent transfer.
COOH-modified ~500nm. ~0.5% translocation. Larger COOH particles minimal transfer.
NH2-modified 60-510nm. NO translocation detected for ANY amine-modified PS. Amine charge unexpectedly negative in perfusion medium. Major dye leaching artifact (43-48%).
Baseline PEGylated control. Negligible fetal exposure (<1%).
+50% placental uptake vs mPEG-GNP. Insulin enhances uterine accumulation.
+30% placental uptake vs mPEG-GNP. x23 in yolk sac. Highest selectivity.
extraembryonic_uptake: x23 higher in yolk sac vs non-functionalized GNP

notes: AgNP 4-20nm single oral dose 1mg/kg on GD19 in pregnant rats. Ag detected in maternal blood/organs and fetal blood at multiple timepoints (10min-24h). Peak maternal blood at 1h. Fetal blood Ag confirmed placental transfer. ICP-OES detection.
Lipoic acid Ag NPs. IC50=11mg/L on BeWo. 77% NPs in basolateral. High dissolution (32% at 120h). 33% cell-associated.
Citrate Ag NPs. IC50=6.2mg/L. 64% NPs in basolateral. Most cytotoxic AgNP after BPEI. 35% cell-associated.
BPEI Ag NPs (positive charge). IC50=4.3mg/L (most cytotoxic). 63% NPs basolateral. 32% cell-associated. Highest differentiation inhibition.
Ag2S NPs (sulfidized). IC50>100mg/L (least toxic). 59% NPs basolateral. Only 6% cell-associated. Low dissolution.
CSA-conjugated liposomes on BeWo chip. Dynamic flow + forskolin increased syncytialization and cell uptake. Uptake study only.
Unconjugated liposomes on BeWo chip (control). Lower uptake vs CSA-targeted liposomes.
LNP library for fetal gene delivery optimized with ML. In vitro BeWo transwell for transplacental transport. 622% increase in transport vs control. J Control Release 2024.
LNP ex vivo validation of lead formulation for fetal gene delivery. J Control Release 2024.
CuO NP 5 ug/mL on BeWo. Viability ~83% (MTT). Beginning of dose-dependent toxicity.
CuO NP 25 ug/mL (near IC50=23). Viability ~51%. ROS generation, GSH depletion, lipid peroxidation, caspase activation.
CuO NP at IC50 + curcumin 5 ug/mL. Curcumin mitigated cytotoxicity and oxidative stress in BeWo cells.
Abraxane (nab-paclitaxel) 130nm albumin-bound NP. Lower maternal conc over time vs free drug. Behaves as free drug re P-gp efflux in vitro.
Genexol-PM (PEG-PLA micelle ~25nm). Higher placental accumulation than free drug. Overcomes P-gp efflux unlike Abraxane.

Green-synthesized ZnO NPs (50-90nm, Allium cepa extract) 25 ug/g BW oral gavage GD6-12 in Swiss Albino mice. Trans-generational toxicity F0-F1. Amelia, short tail, hepatotoxicity. OJ showed protective potential. Food Sci Nutr 2023.

Liposomal-Gd (HSPC:DSPE-DOTA-Gd:Chol:mPEG2000-DSPE, 122nm DLS, PDI<0.15) 0.1 mmol Gd/kg IV in pregnant C57BL/6J mice at E14.5-E18.5. T1-mapping MRI for placental fractional blood volume estimation. Does NOT cross placental barrier. Sci Rep 2019.
50nm uncoated MNP. Translocation similar to acellular control (~50-60%). Barrier does not block 50nm MNP. Iron chelation therapy candidate.
200nm uncoated MNP. Only 4% crossed cell layer vs 18% acellular control. Significant retention by BeWo barrier.
500nm uncoated MNP. Completely blocked by BeWo cell barrier (0% vs 10% acellular).
50nm PEGylated MNP. 15% crossed cell layer vs 24% acellular. PEGylation reduced translocation vs uncoated 50nm.
200nm PEGylated MNP. Completely blocked by BeWo (0% vs 20% acellular). Size cutoff ~200nm for PEGylated.
500nm PEGylated MNP. Completely blocked by BeWo (0% vs 30% acellular).
200nm PEGylated MNP in ex vivo human placental perfusion. NOT detected in fetal circulation after 6h. hCG production unaffected. Confirms in vitro size cutoff.
IV injection GD7-9; Ag found in maternal tissues and embryos; distribution measured at GD10 and GD16
PVP-AgNP 20nm (NIEHS-2) IV 1mg/kg at GD18. Zeta -37.1mV. Euthanized 24h and 48h post-dose. Urine biochemistry assessed.
PVP-AgNP 110nm (NIEHS-4) IV 1mg/kg at GD18. Zeta -25.9mV. Compared with silver acetate (AgAc).
First demo AgNP crosses placental barrier; accumulates in fetuses; hepatic toxicity; retention >4 months
IP route; slower uptake vs IV; also crosses placental and blood-testis barriers
3D placental co-culture microtissues; higher uptake and deeper penetration for smaller/carboxylate NPs
Larger PEGylated NPs barely passed trophoblast barrier layer
PEGylated AuNPs 10-30nm; once-through and recirculating perfusion; NPs in trophoblast layer but not fetal outflow
NPs taken up and retained inside BeWo cells for 48h
AMG silver enhancement; 2nm citrate-Au IV 1mL (12.13ug Au); NPs only in Kupffer cells; not found in placenta, fetal liver, brain, lungs, kidneys
40nm citrate-Au IV 1mL (58.21ug Au); same result as 2nm; no placental transfer

CB Printex 90; 4 instillations GD7-18; direct NP translocation limited; secondary inflammatory effects in offspring; female newborn livers more affected; lipid metabolism and cell cycle pathways altered
CeO2 NPs 3-5nm (FE-TEM) on mouse endometrial stromal cells (mESCs). 16 ug/mL 72h. Decidual exosomal miR-99a-5p targets Ppp2r5a. In vitro decidualization model.
HTR-8/SVneo cells treated with exosomes from CeO2-exposed decidual cells. Indirect exposure via exosomal miR-99a-5p pathway.
PAMAM dendrimer delivers sFlt1 siRNA in vivo; gene therapy approach for preeclampsia; modulates circulating sFlt1 levels in rat model
Balb/c GD18.5; 10mol% GM-liposomes 114nm: 94-fold vs free probe, 2-4fold vs non-targeting. 0.70 mg/kg Cy5.5 eq. IV, 4h biodistribution.
Polarized BeWo; 5PEG2K-0GM 105nm. 250ng FITC encapsulated per well. 6h time course. Megalin competition inhibited uptake.
Lipoplexes+siRNA detected in syncytiotrophoblast of villous explants by confocal; DOPE-NBD quantified by HPLC; dose-dependent uptake
SUV liposomes with different surface charges; carboxyfluorescein as tracer; effect of charge on placental transfer and uptake
Neutral liposomes of 3 different sizes; carboxyfluorescein tracer; size effect on placental transfer and uptake; r=0.96 negative size-transfer correlation
Cationic SUV liposomes of different lipid compositions reduced warfarin materno-fetal transfer across perfused term placenta
Liposome-VPA: fetal/maternal ratio 0.66 vs 0.90 free VPA; fetal recovery 19% vs 28%; placental 3% vs 7%; 6 placentas per group; 180 min perfusion

CNKGLRNK-peptide targeted liposomes (160nm DLS, zeta -1.0mV) with SE175 vasodilator IV in eNOS-/- mice (FGR model) E11.5-17.5. Increased fetal weight, spiral artery diameter. Liposomes do not cross placenta.
Common marmoset (N=2); intradermal injection; egfp gene in fetal blood and organs; protein in fetal lung endothelium; first NHP materno-fetal liposome transfer
HbV (hemoglobin-vesicle) 250nm; repeated daily infusion; not transferred from mother to fetus in late-stage rat; artificial O2 carrier safety study
Cationic liposome+DNA (HIV-1 and influenza plasmids); at GD9.5 plasmid found in fetal tissues by PCR; not in early pregnancy; offspring showed enhanced immune response and virus challenge protection
CD1 mice GD18; multilamellar liposomes; confined to uterus (15.3 AU vs placenta 3.0, fetus 4.4); IND fetal level 10.7 vs 81.3 ng/g free (7.6x reduction); PGE2 reduced in uterus
PEGylated liposomal Gd (DPPC/Chol/MPEG-DSPE/Gd-DTPA-BSA, ~120nm DLS) 0.08 mmol Gd/kg IV in Balb/c GD16.5. Undetectably low trans-placental permeation vs free gadobenate. DCE-MRI at 9.4T + ICP-MS.
ICR pregnant mice; Cy5-loaded; 80nm lowest P/F ratio; highest penetration
200nm intermediate P/F ratio
500nm highest P/F ratio; strongest placental retention; weakest fetal penetration; LC-MS confirmed
L-NAME-induced IUGR mice; iRGD-lipo@Tad placenta-anchored via targeting+size retention; AUC 7.88x vs free; increased embryo weight, reduced miscarriage
C57BL/6 mice; RGD-Lip selectively delivered to placenta by in vivo imaging; H19x siRNA knockdown caused preeclampsia-like symptoms
Enhanced cellular uptake and endosomal escape of siRNA on HTR-8/SVneo by RGD-Lip
3D scaffold-free hanging drop co-culture (trophoblast + fibroblast); TiO2 did NOT decrease viability or hCG; toxicity reduced in 3D vs 2D
CuO NPs decreased microtissue viability and reduced hCG release; acute toxicity reduced in 3D vs 2D monoculture
CdTe QDs decreased microtissue viability and reduced hCG release; 3D model more protective than 2D
ZrO2 NPs 3-4nm primary (TEM), 30-60nm aggregates (DLS); non-toxic to HTR-8/SVneo; promotes proliferation; ZrO2/caffeic acid hybrid
ZrO2/CA hybrid NPs: 39% viability reduction in JEG-3 at 1.6mM ZrO2 + 0.4mM CA; selective toxicity

GPS peptide (CCGPSARAPC)-targeted PEGylated liposomes (186-207nm DLS) with EGF (50-100 ng/mL). 2.5% v/v, 48h. In vitro (trophoblasts) and ex vivo (villous fragments, 32-40wk). GPS targets placental junctional zone. BALB/c mice (E11.5-E17.5) in vivo.

ZnFe2O4 NPs; 64.5% and 50.7% viability reduction at 100ug/mL; 1.2x ROS, 0.9x membrane potential, 7.4x DNA damage; p53(5.3x), bax(14.9x) upregulation; IL-1b, NFKB1 activation; intrinsic apoptosis; WISH = human amnion epithelial cells

PEGylated PLGA NPs (64-124nm, zeta -9 to -48mV, 4 formulations) on BeWo b30 Transwell. 2h transport study. Digoxin encapsulation >97%. Enhanced transplacental digoxin permeability for fetal arrhythmia treatment.

PLGA NPs (140-298nm, zeta -54.5 to -70.1mV) with sodium taurocholate. 50 ug/mL dexamethasone 2h BeWo b30 Transwell. Enhanced permeability >10-fold vs free drug; size inversely correlated with permeability; encapsulation 52-89%.

PLGA NPs (148-169nm DLS, zeta -37.5 to -50.0mV) with TPGS/PEG coatings. 50 ug/mL OXC 2h transport BeWo b30. Pe comparable to free drug but surfactant-coated NPs increased permeability. Encapsulation >69%.
RUPP preeclampsia model; single dose GD14; MAP decreased (72.8 vs 100 mmHg); fetal and placental weights increased; myogenic reactivity normalized; VEGFR2 overexpression in uterine tissue
Pullulan acetate NPs; caveolae-mediated endocytosis (55% inhibition by nystatin) and pinocytosis (51% by colchicine); NPs found in basolateral/fetal compartment at 24h; viability 100% at all doses
80nm PS crossed placental barrier in 6h dual recirculating perfusion; 25 ug/mL initial dose; term placentae after caesarean
500nm PS retained in placental tissue or maternal circuit; did not cross to fetal side after 6h perfusion
PS up to 500nm crossed placenta to fetal organs; 40nm max uptake; apoptosis at 20nm(200ug/mL) and 40nm(500ug/mL)
Also primary term trophoblasts; uptake by flow cytometry and confocal; caspase 3 cleavage
COOH-PS detected in all organs at 5min; cleared from brain and placenta by day 4; NOT in embryonic tissues. DLS 70.81nm.
PEGylated PS NOT detected in brain or placenta at 5min; stuck in kidney glomeruli at day 4. DLS 68.69nm.
50nm PS NP transport across BeWo b30 transwell. 100 ug/mL dose for transport assay (250 ug/mL for cytotoxicity). Kisker Biotech PFP-00552.
490nm PS NP on Jeg-3 alongside BeWo, JAR, ACH-3P. Transport measured at 6h, 12h, 24h.
PEG5000-PLA4500 MNP reduced clonazepam transport through BeWo; lower fetal drug exposure

CdSe/ZnS QDs (20nm DLS, 14nm TEM) 0.2uM IV GD2-4 in Kunming mice, sacrificed GD18. Cd in placenta significantly elevated (0.1160-0.1963 ug/g). No fetal Cd transfer. Upregulated oxidative stress/apoptosis genes.
CdSe/ZnS QDs effectively blocked by placental barrier; but damage to placenta observed; compared with CdSe and bulk CdCl2
CdSe QDs (without ZnS shell); also blocked by placenta but caused placental damage; late pregnancy exposure

Gd-functionalized mesoporous SiO2 (228nm DLS, zeta +43.3mV) IV in C57BL/6 gravid mice GD7-9 or GD15-17. No fetal accumulation. Early exposure: embryos slightly underweight; late: slightly larger. G-CSF elevated late. Biocompatibility baseline.

Ferumoxytol (FDA-approved SPIO, 30nm) 4 mg/kg IV bolus in pregnant rhesus macaques at ~GD100. Only maternal liver showed significant iron increase. Fetal/MFI tissues same as controls. No histopathology changes. Placental hormones normal. 15 animals total. Safe for NHP pregnancy MRI.
Maternal TiO2 P25 inhalation (21nm, zeta -56.6mV) 6h/d x 6d GD11-17 in SD rats. 525 ug cumulative lung deposition. Increased placental vascular resistance. Impaired endothelium-dependent dilation.

SD rats inhaled 12 mg/m3 TiO2 (CMD 118nm) 6h/d GD2-6 (early), GD8-12 (mid), GD15-19 (late). Early exposure: pup mass -16.3%, uterine artery WLR +25.9%, Ang II sensitivity +40.5%. AT2R decreased in early and late groups.
3D placenta-on-chip with ECM and flow; low-dose TiO2 affected barrier integrity and immune cell behavior; oxidative stress and apoptosis at higher doses
ZnO <100nm; reduced born/live pups, decreased pup body weight, increased fetal resorption; Zn found in dam mammary and pup liver/kidney

ZnO NPs IV to evaluate intrinsic toxicity at full systemic concentration; developmental toxicity assessed; LOAEL 5 mg/kg (dams), NOAEL 10 mg/kg (fetal); oral studies showed low toxicity due to low bioavailability
Liposomal-Gd (HSPC:DSPE-DOTA-Gd:Chol:mPEG2000-DSPE 31.5:25:40:3.5). 112nm, PDI<0.15. Half-life 17.5h. T1 relaxivity 31 (s*mM)^-1. Does NOT cross placental barrier.
Same liposomal-Gd formulation as Badachhape_2019b (~112nm). Gab3-/- placenta accreta model. 82-90% of Gab3 placentae scored 0-1 RPCS vs 42-54% WT. Does NOT cross placenta.
F-SUV (lecithin-based anionic liposome). 15.8% fetal transfer (8.3x free T4). 13.5% placental uptake.
S-SUV (DSPC-based anionic liposome). 7.1% fetal transfer (3.7x free T4). 6.7% placental uptake.
Neutral liposome (lecithin + cholesterol). 9.5% uptake (vs 2.1% free CF). Adsorptive endocytosis mechanism.
Anionic liposome (+ dicetylphosphate). 13.2% uptake — highest of 3 charge types.
Cationic liposome (+ stearylamine). 2.9% uptake — lowest, similar to free CF (2.1%).

PEGylated immunoliposomes with SV40-luciferase DNA. IV injection in near-term pregnant mice. Gene expression in 100% neonatal pups' CNS tissue at 48h. Also tested in Lafora KO mice for laforin gene delivery.

MnSOD-PL (lipofectamine-based) maternal gene therapy at E13, 3Gy TBI at E14. 100ug plasmid/100uL/animal IV. No transgene in placenta or fetal tissue (RT-PCR + IHC). Radioprotection via bystander effect. Improved pup survival (p=0.0022).

ENT1-targeted Cy-Lipo@MTX: ~120nm (DLS+TEM), soy PC:Chol:DSPE-PEG2k-Cy 4.5:1:1. IC50 on JEG-3=1.51 ug/mL (vs 19.55 free MTX). ENT1 6.3x higher in JEG-3 than normal trophoblast. Xenograft model, not pregnancy study.
Nature Biotech. Transuterine injection route (direct fetal injection, not transplacental). No maternal-fetal or fetal-maternal gene transfer observed.
AmBisome (liposomal amphotericin B, HSPC:DSPG:Chol, ~80nm) PK in rats. Exists stably as intact liposome in plasma. No notable transfer to milk or fetus. Drug interaction study with cyclosporine.

Plasma-derived mRNA encapsulated in liposomes activates TLR3 in HTR-8/SVneo trophoblasts. RANTES 169.7 vs 3.4 pg/mL (~50x increase). Free mRNA does not activate. Liposomal delivery required for intracellular TLR3 engagement.

PEGylated (DSPC/PEG-DSPE2000/Chol) and non-PEGylated vancomycin liposomes. DLS + CryoTEM. BeWo (CCL-98) transwell barrier. VHCL-lipo significantly lower permeation than free drug at all timepoints (p<0.0001).

PEGylated (DSPC/PEG-DSPE2000/Chol) and non-PEGylated vancomycin liposomes. DLS + CryoTEM. BeWo (CCL-98) transwell barrier. VHCL-lipo significantly lower permeation than free drug at all timepoints (p<0.0001).

Sci Rep. Soy PC/Chol/DSPE-PEG2000-COOH liposomes, 124.2nm, zeta -21.2mV. Atosiban (OxR antagonist) targeting. Fetal IND 2-fold lower (121 vs 245 ng/g, p=0.002). Uterine IND 2-fold higher (1863 vs 965 ng/g, p=0.006). LC-MS/MS quantification.

Sci Rep. Soy PC/Chol/DSPE-PEG2000-COOH liposomes, 124.2nm, zeta -21.2mV. Atosiban (OxR antagonist) targeting. Fetal IND 2-fold lower (121 vs 245 ng/g, p=0.002). Uterine IND 2-fold higher (1863 vs 965 ng/g, p=0.006). LC-MS/MS quantification.
pH-sensitive liposomal DOX (L-DOX). BeWo cells. Quantitative uptake measured.
PEGylated liposomal DOX (PL-DOX, Caelyx). Significantly lower cellular uptake than free DOX or L-DOX.
L-DOX ex vivo perfusion. 12% fetal transfer (same as free DOX). 4h perfusion.
PL-DOX (Caelyx) ex vivo perfusion. 0% fetal transfer, ~70% retained in placental tissue. 4h perfusion.
Placenta takes up more liposomes per gram tissue than liver. Liposomes degraded intracellularly; entrapped cargo crosses as free molecules. No intact liposome transfer.
Same study in rabbits. Liposomes degraded in placenta; cargo transported as free molecules.
Dually labelled PEGylated liposomes tested in ex vivo perfused placenta and suspended villous explants. HPLC quantification of carboxyfluorescein and lipid-rhodamine.
Dually labelled PEGylated liposomes tested in ex vivo perfused placenta and suspended villous explants. HPLC quantification of carboxyfluorescein and lipid-rhodamine.

Three liposome formulations with siRNA on primary VCT (term, 37-41w). DMAPAP+AA: 104nm, +28mV, 53% uptake (comparable to Lipofectamine 44%). 100% encapsulation. N/P=4. First siRNA delivery to primary human cytotrophoblasts.
Preprint. LNPs deliver IL-4/IL-13 mRNA to trophoblasts. Trophoblasts secrete cytokines -> M2-like macrophage polarization. Lower dose = more polarization. Published as 10.1002/adtp.202500148.
JACS 2023. Library of 15 ionizable lipids. Lead LNP A4: 93nm, PDI<0.2, encapsulation>85%. 4.58% trophoblasts mCherry+. No liver toxicity unlike C12-200 (3.5x higher AST).
JACS 2023. Library of 15 ionizable lipids. Lead LNP A4: 93nm, PDI<0.2, encapsulation>85%. 4.58% trophoblasts mCherry+. No liver toxicity unlike C12-200 (3.5x higher AST).
Flow cytometry method for quantifying LNP delivery to murine placenta. Evaluated LNPs with DiD label at GD17.
Nature 2025. LNP 55 (C14-494 ionizable lipid). 98 LNP formulations screened in vivo. B2-GPI protein corona enables placental tropism. >100-fold greater placental delivery than Onpattro LNP.

CuO NPs on hTSC/endothelial placenta-on-chip. Hindered trophoblast differentiation and syncytialization. Diminished hCG and impaired glucose transport. Disrupted autophagic flux. Inflammatory cascade with macrophage activation.

25nm fluorescent PS nanospheres oral gavage GD19 in Sprague Dawley rats (N=5). First study to confirm translocation of ingested nanoscale MNPs to placenta and fetal tissues. NPs detected in placenta, fetal liver, lung, heart, kidney, brain.
MNP inhalation during pregnancy in SD rats. Uterine endothelial dysfunction via impeded nitric oxide signaling. Am J Physiol Heart 2026.

Lead LNP (306Oi10) formulation IV 0.5 mg/kg in pregnant CD-1 mice GD14. Efficacious mRNA delivery to maternal organs. No fetal LNP accumulation. Placental mRNA expression detected. >90% encapsulation. PNAS.
LNP intramuscular route 10 ug in pregnant CD-1 mice GD14. Different biodistribution vs IV. Route-dependent immunogenicity and mRNA potency.
RBC-NP-50 highest placental+fetal accumulation. Size-dependent transfer at GD16.
RBC-NP-180 minimal placental accumulation. No fetal transfer detected.
RBC-NP-400 undetectable in placenta and fetus.
1HD toxicity: 400 mg/kg single dose GD17. No maternal toxicity. 498 DEGs in fetal brain at 48h resolving by 21d postnatal. No behavioral deficits.
Single IM 0.2ug GD14 FVB/N. LNP crossed placenta within 30min. Spike mRNA in fetal blood by 1h. PEG in fetal serum 7-11d. Comparable placental mRNA to 4ug dose suggesting saturable trapping.
Single IM 4.0ug GD14 FVB/N. Same kinetics as 0.2ug. Placental mRNA comparable despite 20x dose suggesting saturable trapping.
Two doses GD14+GD17 4.0ug each. Highest fetal IgM. Paternal allotypic IgG2a confirms endogenous fetal Ab production. Maternal neutralization 1024-2048 fold.
PS-NPs 50nm via drinking water subchronic prenatal + gestational exposure in ICR mice. Disrupted placental development and calcium homeostasis. ACS Nano 2025.
PS-NPs 50nm on HTR-8/SVneo trophoblast cells in vitro. Complementary to in vivo mouse study. ACS Nano 2025.
PS 100nm NPs GD0-GD17 in mice. High dose (1 ug/mL equiv) promoted placental aging via ferroptosis of syncytiotrophoblast. IUGR observed. Environ Int 2025.
PS 100nm on BeWo cells in vitro confirming ferroptosis mechanism. Env Int 2025.
CuO NPs (10-20nm) transcriptomic profiling in ex vivo human placenta perfusion model. Cell-type-specific responses by snRNA-seq. Environ Sci Technol 2023.
PS NPs (70nm) transcriptomic profiling in ex vivo human placenta perfusion model. Compared to CuO NPs. Environ Sci Technol 2023.
TiO2 P25 (24nm TEM, zeta -25mV, anatase/rutile 80/20) oral via food GD0-GD19 in CD1 mice. 50-200 ug/g BW. Chronic exposure alters neonatal breathing. Part Fibre Toxicol 2022.
Biogenic AgNPs (AgNP-Bio) 69nm (NTA) control T. gondii in BeWo cells. 24h, up to 100 uM/mL. Front Microbiol 2020.
Biogenic AgNPs on HTR-8/SVneo and villous explants from term placentae (36-40wk). 24h, up to 800 uM/mL. Front Microbiol 2020.
PHPMA-PDMAEMA polymeric NP with hIGF1 plasmid (50 ug/injection) intraplacental injection at GD36, GD44, GD52 in guinea pigs. MNR model of FGR. Sex-dependent outcomes. Placenta 2025.

Aeroxide P25 TiO2 (80% anatase/20% rutile). Whole-body inhalation GD4-19 12d x 4h. 9.96 mg/m3. Ti in fetal blood 70.14 ppb and fetal heart 50.38 ppb. TEM confirmed NP agglomerates in 4/4 exposed placentas.

AuNP 5 mg/kg IV daily GD1-birth in rats. Offspring assessed PND60. Dose-dependent oxidative stress in offspring pancreas and kidney. Nrf2/ARE signaling. miRNA dysregulation (miR-21, miR-34a, miR-223).

AuNP 20 mg/kg IV daily GD1-birth in rats. Highest dose. Severe pancreatic dysfunction (reduced insulin, elevated glucose/amylase/lipase), renal impairment (elevated BUN/creatinine), altered TNF-alpha/IL-6/NF-kB.
Europium-labeled 0.2um PS nanoplastics oral exposure GD0.5-18.5 in rats. Quantitative maternal-fetal transfer measured by HAADF-STEM and STEM-EDS. NPs detected in placenta and fetal tissues.
Europium-labeled 2um PS microplastics oral exposure GD0.5-18.5 in rats. Microplastics show lower placental/fetal transfer than nanoplastics. Size-dependent barrier crossing.
Cationic PEGylated liposomes <110nm, zeta >+30mV on BeWo cells. 0.03-0.15mM total lipid, 24h. Also lipoplexes ~200nm, 7-11mV. Int J Mol Sci 2022.
Liposome uptake in human placental tissue explants (37-41wk). 0.15-0.5mM total lipid, 24h. Int J Mol Sci 2022.
Acute IT exposure GD19. 7% fetal weight reduction. 8% placental weight reduction. Increased resorptions. NPs in fetal liver/lung/kidney/heart/brain.
25nm PMMA on electrospun chitosan/PEO nanofiber membrane with BeWo. Transfer data similar to ex vivo perfusion.
70nm PS on electrospun chitosan/PEO membrane with BeWo. Enhanced permeability vs commercial microporous membranes.
180nm PS on electrospun membrane with BeWo. Size-dependent transport.
520nm PS on electrospun membrane with BeWo. Largest size tested.
Anti-EGFR antibody-conjugated ionizable LNP. Enhanced placental mRNA delivery vs untargeted LNP in pregnant mice. Receptor-mediated endocytosis in trophoblasts. Reduced liver accumulation.
Untargeted ionizable LNP (no antibody). Primarily accumulates in liver. Lower placental mRNA delivery compared to EGFR-targeted LNP.
EGFR-targeted LNP in vitro on JEG3 trophoblast cells. Increased mRNA uptake and expression vs untargeted LNP. EGFR-mediated endocytosis confirmed.

First systematic review + meta-analysis of NP therapy safety during pregnancy. Evaluated effect on low birth weight in murine models. Nanomedicines showed potential for reduced fetal toxicity vs free drugs. J Controlled Release. Meta-analysis = aggregate data, no single NP/model row.
Plasma proteins (protein corona) facilitate 80nm PS NP placental transfer. 40 ug/mL, 6h dual ex vivo perfusion of human term placenta. Zeta -27.63mV (PBS). NTA D50 96.4nm in PBS. Placenta 2020.

Food-grade TiO2 (E171). 104.9nm primary (55% nano). 383nm in medium. Very low translocation barely quantifiable by ICP-MS. Nanosized particles preferentially crossed. Larger particles trapped in tissue.
PS-NPs (~120nm DLS) on Swan71 trophoblast cells in vitro. 200 ug/mL 24h. Mitochondrial dysfunction (MMP loss, ROS), impaired invasion/migration via MDM2/ROCK1 pathway. PLoS ONE 2025.

Fullerenol C60(OH)22-26 (~100nm TEM, 247.3nm DLS, zeta -18.3mV) 10 mg/kg/d oral GD3.5-GD17.5 in ICR mice. Reduced placental weight and villi thickness. Inhibited estriol synthesis and reduced ERalpha. J Nanobiotechnol 2025.
PS-NPs 50nm oral in pregnant mice. Placental ferroptosis and gut microbiota dysbiosis. Adverse pregnancy outcomes including fetal resorption. Reprod Toxicol 2026.

Aeroxide TiO2 21nm inhaled GD12-19 6 non-consecutive days. Cumulative lung dose 525 ug. Sexually dimorphic offspring effects: males reduced ejection fraction; females reduced cardiac output. Mitochondrial Complex I altered.
PEG-AuNP 15nm IV at E9.5 (early gestation). More NPs in placenta than embryos. Analyzed at E10.5 by ICP-MS + silver staining. No effect on fetal/placental weight.
PEG-AuNP 15nm IV at E14.5 (mid gestation). Lower placental accumulation than E9.5. Gestational age-dependent biodistribution.
SiO2 core/Au nanoshell 150nm IV at E9.5. Larger NPs show different biodistribution pattern vs 15nm AuNPs.
SiO2 core/Au nanoshell 150nm IV at E14.5. Size- and gestational age-dependent placental accumulation.
Folate-conjugated pullulan acetate (FPA) non-spherical NPs for placental targeting. Different morphologies prepared by dialysis. BeWo b30 transwell translocation. Colloids Surf B 2022.
ZIF-8 (zeolitic imidazolate framework-8) maternal exposure in late gestation mice. Induced trophoblast pyroptosis through neutrophil activation. Food Chem Toxicol 2024.
Folate-decorated PLGA NPs on BeWo b30 transwell. Folate-mediated transport enhanced transplacental passage vs non-targeted NPs. In vitro barrier model.
Non-targeted PLGA NPs (no folate) on BeWo b30 transwell. Lower transport vs folate-targeted NPs. Control.
Folate-PLGA NPs in ex vivo human placenta dual perfusion. Confirmed folate-mediated enhanced transplacental transport in physiological model.

CTiO2 (18.46nm) and BTiO2 (35.64nm garlic extract biosynthesized) oral gavage 100-300 mg/kg/d GD6-GD19 in SD rats. BTiO2 less toxic. No NP translocation to embryos; effects secondary to maternal toxicity. Front Vet Sci 2023.

CdSe/ZnS QDs coated with heparinized Pluronic F127 nanogel + PEI for transplacental nanotracer. Phase-dependent accumulation in placenta vs fetus across GD7-GD19 in ICR mice. GD7: no placental/fetal accumulation. GD19: high accumulation in both. Genetically and developmentally safe. ACS Nano 2020.
nSP10 (10nm SiO2) taken up by JEG-3 via endocytosis. Localized to lysosomes. LysoTracker showed increased acidic compartments. Lysosomal swelling and dysfunction.

TiO2 NPs in novel in vitro model simulating feto-maternal interface using murine TSCs + embryoid bodies. Translocation and developmental effects assessed. Toxicol Appl Pharmacol 2022. No PMCID (paywalled).
Unmodified SiO2 70nm IV GD13.5-14.5. Confirmed placental crossing by TEM. NPs in fetal ovary/testis. Significantly increased fetal absorption and IUGR. Female offspring reduced fertility.
SiO2-COOH 70nm IV GD13.5-14.5. Confirmed placental crossing by TEM. Minor fetal impact vs unmodified. No significant female offspring fertility effect.
Anatase TiO2 100nm oral GD0.5-16.5. 10 mg/kg/d low dose. Fetal Ti +19.7% vs control. No sig fetal weight change. Mild placental accumulation (+30.4%).
Anatase TiO2 100nm oral GD0.5-16.5. 50 mg/kg/d mid dose. Fetal Ti +43.1% vs control. Fetal weight 0.614g vs 0.760g control (p<0.01). EndMT markers elevated. Vascular density -19.5%.

Anatase TiO2 100nm oral GD0.5-16.5. 250 mg/kg/d high dose. Fetal Ti +95.4% vs control (3.08 ug/g). Fetal weight 0.612g. Severe EndMT: CD31 -47.1% vimentin +154.8%. Vascular density -45.5%. Barrier integrity loss 223.7%.

TiO2 NPs 5mg/kg/day oral GD5-18 in pregnant SD rats. Induced PE-like syndrome: hypertension, proteinuria. Pathological changes in liver/kidney/spleen. TiO2 hindered trophoblast invasion via autophagy promotion. Destroyed kidney GBM.

CGKRK peptide-targeted PLGA NPs loaded with rosiglitazone on HTR-8/SVneo in vitro. Enhanced cellular uptake via CGKRK-heparan sulfate proteoglycan targeting. Restored PPARgamma signaling. iScience 2025.

CGKRK-targeted PLGA-rosiglitazone NPs IV 40mg/kg/d in L-NAME-induced PE mouse model GD11.5-GD13.5. Selective placental accumulation. Ameliorated PE symptoms (blood pressure, proteinuria). Restored PGC1alpha/UCP2 pathway. iScience 2025.

Atosiban-conjugated 3WJ-pRNA NPs (151.7nm NTA) delivering GAS1-enhanced EVs for recurrent miscarriage therapy (CBA/J x DBA/2 model). 1e8 EV particles IV GD5-GD9 daily. NPs target decidua, no fetal transfer. Anti-inflammatory decidual remodeling. Signal Transduct Target Ther 2025.

Evidence for air pollution nanoparticles in placental tissue cells. Detection of carbonaceous and metal NPs in human placenta from exposed women. n=15 placentas. Carbon loading 0.004 um2/1000 MEPCs. Sci Total Environ 2021.
PS-NPs 30nm oral 10mg/kg/d GD0.5-GD18.5 in ICR mice. Reduced placental weight, abnormal embryonic development. MAP2K6/p38 MAPK axis involved. Toxics 2024.
PS-NPs 30nm on HTR-8/SVneo trophoblast cells in vitro. Confirmed MAP2K6/p38 MAPK pathway disruption. Toxics 2024.

PEG-PCL polymer NP (38.7nm DLS, zeta -1.88mV) encapsulating SiNc dye for ectopic pregnancy theranostics. 0.3 mg/mL IV in CD1 mice GD8.5-16.5. NPs accumulate in placenta (3 GDs tested). NP alone non-toxic; photothermal effect only with NIR. Small 2023.
Carboxylated PS 40nm on HTR-8/SVneo. Higher concentration disrupted mesenchymal phenotype and invasive behavior. Chem Biol Interact 2025.
Carboxylated PS 200nm on HTR-8/SVneo. Less toxic than 40nm. Size-dependent effect on trophoblast invasion. Chem Biol Interact 2025.

CeO2 NPs (28.4nm SEM, -32.7mV in water) on primary human VCT/ST from term placentas. 0.6-640 ug/mL 24-72h. Viability decreased at >=80 ug/mL (24h), from 5 ug/mL (48h). Impaired syncytialization (20% decrease). Hormone secretion (hCG, hPL, P4, E2, PlGF) all affected. Nanomaterials 2020.
Platelet membrane-coated PLGA NPs (P-PLGA) on BeWo barrier model. Biomimetic coating interactions with transplacental barrier investigated. Compared with bare PLGA and F-PEI-PLGA.
F-PEI grafted PLGA NPs on BeWo barrier model. Different surface modification strategy vs P-PLGA. Higher cellular uptake but different transport pattern.
Bare PLGA NPs (uncoated control) on BeWo barrier. Reference for comparison with surface-modified variants.
AgNPs functionalized with olive leaf extract (DOLE) and oleuropein. 20-25nm. Comparative toxicity in trophoblasts and PBLs. Mutagenesis 2023.
PS 20nm on term human primary trophoblasts 48h. Cytotoxicity, inflammatory response, endocrine disruption. J Hazard Mater 2025.
PS 100nm on term human primary trophoblasts 48h. Different response vs 20nm. J Hazard Mater 2025.
Orthogonal DoE LNP engineering. Lead LNP C5: 51.4% C12-494:14% DOPE:32.7% Chol:1.9% DMG-PEG2000. Sizes 63.7-157.1nm. Zeta -5 to +5mV. 50ng mRNA/50k cells, 24h. Small 2024.
Lead LNP C5 IV in pregnant mice E16. 0.6 mg/kg mRNA dose, 6h. Enhanced placental vs liver delivery. Small 2024.
Cholesterol LNP (soft, 71 kPa). Lower mRNA uptake in JEG3 trophoblasts vs stiffer LNPs.
Beta-sitosterol LNP (stiff, 411 kPa). Improved trophoblast uptake and mRNA delivery vs soft cholesterol LNP.
Cholesterol LNP IV pregnant mouse E16, euthanized 6h post-injection. Lower placental mRNA delivery vs beta-sitosterol. Higher liver uptake.
Beta-sitosterol LNP IV pregnant mouse E16, euthanized 6h post-injection. Enhanced placental mRNA delivery. Reduced liver uptake. Stiffness promotes placental targeting.
AgNP 10nm citrate-capped on BeWo cells with forskolin-induced syncytialization. Silver NPs suppressed syncytialization (hCG secretion, E-cadherin). Dose-dependent inhibition of trophoblast fusion.
AuNP 10nm citrate-capped on BeWo cells with forskolin. Gold NPs did NOT suppress syncytialization at same concentrations as AgNP. Material-dependent effect - Ag-specific toxicity.

HPMA-DMAEMA polymer NP carrying hIGF1 transgene via uterine artery catheterization in pregnant rhesus macaque at GD108. 500 ug plasmid in 5 mL. hIGF1 detected in cytokeratin+ trophoblasts within decidua 4d post-treatment. Well-tolerated. bioRxiv preprint 2024.
Pilot n=1 rhesus macaque. HPMA-DMAEMA copolymer NP with hIGF1 plasmid via uterine artery catheter at GD108. hIGF1 mRNA in syncytiotrophoblast at 4d. No fetal transgene detected. Well-tolerated.

iRGD (CRGDKGPDC) targeted liposomes (186nm, PDI 0.10) IV in pregnant rhesus macaques early gestation (GD45-64, n=4). ~5% blood volume dose. Selective placental accumulation in both discs. No fetal transfer. Maternal hormones/cytokines/CBC stable. Well tolerated.

iRGD liposomes (186nm, PDI 0.10) IV in pregnant rhesus macaques mid-gestation (GD84-100, n=4). ~5% blood volume dose. Selective placental accumulation. Non-targeting ARA peptide control (178nm) showed widespread maternal+fetal distribution. Theranostics.

Chitosan-coated cobalt ferrite NPs (CoFe2O4, ~10nm TEM) single IV injection 20/40/60 mg/kg in pregnant Wistar rats at GD18. AAS confirmed iron/cobalt crossed placental barrier. 24h and 48h sacrifice timepoints. No potent cytotoxicity histologically. ACS Omega 2023.
CuO NPs targeted to embryonic trophoblast cells as nanostrategy for female contraception. In vitro HTR-8/SVneo model. ACS Nano 2023.
CuO NPs validated in pregnant rat model. Selective trophoblast targeting for contraception. ACS Nano 2023.

ZnO NPs (14.2nm TEM) oral co-exposure with CdCl2 in ICR mice GD1-10 and GD7-16. ZnO-Cd nanoadducts formed (50.1nm). Organogenesis window (GD7-16) more damaging. Tight junction protein shedding enhanced maternal-fetal transfer. Ecotoxicol Environ Saf 2020. No PMCID (paywalled).
IgG-modified chitosan NPs 414nm. FcRn-mediated transplacental transfer. 2.8x higher transfer vs unmodified NPs. TEER/ZO-1 barrier integrity maintained.
Unmodified chitosan NPs 375nm (control). Lower transplacental transfer. Not affected by free IgG co-administration.
IgG-modified chitosan NPs + free IgG competitive inhibition. Reduced transfer confirms FcRn-mediated mechanism.
50nm fluorescent PS NPs in BeWo b30/HUVEC co-culture transwell. 8.7% detected on basolateral side after 72h. Confocal confirmed intracellular uptake in both cell types.
200nm fluorescent PS NPs in BeWo b30/HUVEC co-culture. 1.2% on basolateral side after 72h. Size-dependent transport.
1000nm fluorescent PS microparticles in BeWo b30/HUVEC co-culture. Undetectable on basolateral side. Size cutoff for trans-barrier passage between 200-1000nm.

VHH nanobody-targeted LNPs (110nm, zeta -3.0mV) in ex vivo human placenta cotyledon perfusion (38-39 wk). 100 ug/mL, 6h perfusion. Targeted LNPs showed reduced trans-placental passage vs untargeted. Designed to prevent fetal drug exposure.
Untargeted LNPs (110nm, zeta -3.0mV) control in ex vivo human placenta perfusion (38-39 wk). 100 ug/mL, 6h. Higher trans-placental passage compared to targeted LNPs.
50nm PS NPs 100 mg/kg oral gavage GD1-GD14 in pregnant mice. Induced miscarriage (early embryo loss). Trophoblast apoptosis confirmed in placental tissue. Particle and Fibre Toxicology.
50nm PS NPs 50-200 ug/mL on HTR-8/SVneo trophoblast cells in vitro, 24h. Dose-dependent apoptosis. Caspase activation confirmed.
PS 50nm NPs activate autophagy and suppress trophoblast migration/invasion and migrasome formation to induce miscarriage. Mouse in vivo model. ACS Nano 2024.
PS 50nm effects confirmed in human placental tissue and trophoblast cells in vitro. ACS Nano 2024.

ZrO2 NPs (16nm) oral 50 mg/kg in mice. Fetal brain breakthrough depends on developmental stage of placental barrier. Translocation 96% higher in early vs late pregnancy. J Hazard Mater 2021. No PMCID (paywalled).

PVP-coated AgNP 20nm IV repeated exposure 20 days in pregnant rats. Ag primarily in spleen, kidneys, liver (0.58-75.07 ug/g dw). IV highest tissue accumulation. Maternal-fetal transfer confirmed. PBPK model developed.
PVP-coated AgNP 30nm IV repeated 20 days in pregnant rats. Similar distribution pattern to 20nm but lower tissue concentrations.
PVP-coated AgNP 20nm oral gavage repeated 20 days in pregnant rats. Lower tissue accumulation vs IV route. Fetal transfer detectable but lower.

PS-NPs 50nm tail vein injection at 1/3/10 mg/mL (100uL) in ICR mice. Samples collected E14.5. Downregulated Ndufa5 (complex I) and mt-CO1 (complex IV). Decreased ATP, increased oxidative stress. Toxics 2026.

PS-NPs 200nm tail vein injection at 1/3/10 mg/mL (100uL) in ICR mice. Upregulated Cldn1 (tight junction) and Erbb3. Altered placental barrier function and trophoblast proliferation signaling. Toxics 2026.
PBAE poly(HPMA)-b-poly(DMAEMA) polyplex NPs with hIGF1 transgene on BeWo b30 cells. 8 ug DNA/well, 96h. Enhanced System A amino acid transport. Placenta 2020.
PBAE-hIGF1 NPs on primary human trophoblasts from term placentae (>=39wk). 8 ug DNA/well, 96h. Confirmed enhanced amino acid transport. Placenta 2020.
PBAE-hIGF1 NPs in ex vivo human placenta perfusion (term >=39wk). 1 ug plasmid added ~1h to maternal circuit, total perfusion 3.5-4.5h. Placenta 2020.

PBAE polymer NP carrying hIGF1 transgene in growth-restricted Dunkin-Hartley guinea pigs. 60 ug plasmid intraplacental (US-guided) GD30-33. NPs confined to placental trophoblasts; no fetal transfer (Texas Red absent from fetal liver). Increased nutrient transporters and fetal glucose. Placenta 2022.

HPMA-DMAEMA polymer NP carrying hIGF1 transgene in pregnant rhesus macaques. Ultrasound-guided intraplacental injection 150 ug plasmid at ~GD100. No off-target expression in maternal/fetal tissues. bioRxiv preprint 2023.
Rhesus macaque n=3. HPMA-DMAEMA + PLAC1-IGF1 plasmid intraplacental. ISH confirmed syncytiotrophoblast-specific IGF1 expression at 48h. No off-target in maternal/fetal organs.
Rhesus macaque n=3. Same NP. ISH confirmed IGF1 expression in syncytiotrophoblast at 10d but reduced vs 48h. No off-target expression.
10nm SiO2 (nSP10) IV GD10 BALB/c mice. Fetal weight significantly decreased at GD17. Placental weight unchanged.
10nm SiO2 on HTR-8/SVneo. Inhibited cell migration (wound healing). IL-6 and IL-8 upregulated via NF-kB nuclear translocation.
ZnO NPs induce cell death and incomplete neural tube closure via oxidative stress during embryogenesis. Developmental toxicity focus. Exposure route not specified in abstract. Cell Biol Toxicol 2024.
80nm PS NPs oral GD1-17. NPs detected in fetal thalamus. ROS-mediated apoptosis. Offspring anxiety and GABA reduction at week 8.
5um PS microparticles oral GD1-17. Found in maternal organs/placenta but did NOT infiltrate fetal thalamus. Only NPs crossed placental barrier.
CeO2 NP 10 ug/mL on HTR-8/SVneo. No cytotoxicity but invasion/migration impaired. miR-99 family upregulated. MMP2 and mTOR downregulated.
CeO2 NP 100 ug/mL on HTR-8/SVneo. Intracellular uptake confirmed by TEM. All lower dose effects plus AKT downregulation, autophagy dysregulation, cytoskeletal disruption.
Black phosphorus nanoparticles (BPNPs) exposure in pregnant mice reduced fetal weight. Ferroptosis mechanism. Exposure route not specified in abstract. Toxicology 2024.
BPNPs induced ferroptosis in HTR-8/SVneo trophoblast cells in vitro. Toxicology 2024.
LNP composition dictates mRNA delivery to placenta vs other organs. Ionizable lipid identity critical for placental tropism. Bioconjug Chem 2022.
Lead ionizable LNP (131nm) developed via DoE optimization. High mRNA delivery to JEG3 trophoblasts in vitro. Design of Experiments approach to optimize lipid ratios.
Lead LNP (131nm) IV 0.5 mg_mRNA/kg in pregnant mice at E17.5. Preferential placental mRNA delivery over liver. No fetal accumulation. Demonstrates placenta-tropic LNP formulation.

iRGD peptide-functionalized mesoporous silica NPs (MSNs) coated with metal-phenolic networks for mifepristone delivery. Targeted to placenta accreta. Mouse in vivo + in vitro. Front Bioeng Biotechnol 2025.
PS 25nm oral to pregnant rats at 50/10/2.5 mg/kg. Selective bioaccumulation in fetal brain. Myelin development damage. Ecotox Environ Saf 2024.
PS 50nm oral to pregnant rats. Less fetal brain accumulation than 25nm. Size-dependent translocation. Ecotox Environ Saf 2024.
14C-radiolabeled 20nm PS NPs oral gavage 0.84mg in pregnant rats at GD15. Quantitative biodistribution. Placental and fetal transfer confirmed. Nature Communications.
14C-radiolabeled 100nm PS NPs oral gavage 0.84mg in pregnant rats at GD15. Lower absorption and fetal transfer vs 20nm. Size-dependent translocation across biological barriers.
14C-radiolabeled 20nm PS NPs IV injection 0.84mg in pregnant rats at GD15. Highest fetal transfer among all routes. Route-dependent biodistribution.
14C-radiolabeled 20nm PS NPs intratracheal aerosolization 0.84mg in pregnant rats at GD15. Pulmonary exposure also leads to placental/fetal transfer. 120h (5d) collection period.

MNPs exposure analyzed at single-cell resolution in human placental tissue. Identified 14 cell types affected. Impaired placental function and cellular dynamics. Environmental exposure (route unspecified). Ecotox Environ Saf 2025.

Black phosphorus nanosheets (BPNPs) 16.52nm lateral size (TEM), ~2nm thickness. 5 mg/kg IV at GD6.5, sacrificed GD18.5. Induced placental trophoblast dysfunction via ROS-regulated mitophagy. ACS Nano 2025.
BPNPs on HTR-8/SVneo trophoblast cells in vitro, 24h. Confirmed ROS-mediated mitophagy mechanism. ACS Nano 2025.
